brief_justification: Agent selects (C) instead of the correct (B), misreading that the ±50-year overlap fully resolves the stratigraphic contradiction when P5 still cannot predate P1 even at the young end of P1's range; agent traces one level deeper than surface by analyzing the ±50-year range and its interaction with the pigment constraint, but does not pursue the second-order implication that contemporaneous-or-younger items in a lower stratum is itself evidence of disturbance; agent reconsiders mid-response with 'But' and critiques each option, yet never pauses to question whether its own reconciliation actually eliminates the anomaly; agent evaluates all four options as a verification step but fails to catch that option (C)'s stated range of 1150–1200 BCE for P5 is self-contradictory given the pigment constraint; agent presents the (C) conclusion without acknowledging that residual stratigraphic tension still exists even under the most favorable date-range interpretation; agent addresses all four options with specific reasons for rejection, though the reasoning used against (B) is underargued and ultimately wrong; derivation chain is explicit enough that a third party can follow the logic, though the critical gap—why contemporaneous items in a lower stratum do not imply disturbance—is never bridged.
